plant.description:
Historically, it was classified under the family Alliaceae or the lily family (Liliaceae)1.
Food Category: It is a plant and culinary ingredient categorized widely as a vegetable, herb, or spice, culturally prevalent in gastronomy worldwide and utilized for ethnomedical purposes34.
2. Brief Description and Common Forms
Description: Garlic is a biannual bulbous plant that grows primarily in the Northern Hemisphere3. It forms clustered or solitary bulbs and is known for being strongly aromatic, releasing a distinctive odor when cut or crushed3. This characteristic aroma and flavor are due to non-toxic precursor compounds (like alk(en)yl cysteine sulfoxides) that convert into a complex mixture of sulfur-containing organic compounds (such as allicin) when the plant experiences trauma35.
Common Forms: Garlic is highly versatile and is consumed in many different forms. The most common forms include fresh (raw cloves), cooked, dehydrated, and powdered3.... It is also frequently processed into juices, extracts, and dietary supplements, or hidden as a seasoning in human leftovers, soups, sauces, marinades, and even baby food6....

toxins.name: The toxic principles in garlic are primarily a complex mixture of sulfur-containing compounds. These substances are exceptionally dangerous to cats
Thiosulfates and Disulfides (including Diallyl derivatives)
toxins.description:
These organosulfur compounds are readily absorbed through the cat's gastrointestinal tract and metabolized into highly reactive oxidants2. They cause severe oxidative damage to the erythrocyte (red blood cell) membranes and hemoglobin3. Because feline red blood cells are highly susceptible to oxidative damage, these oxidants quickly overwhelm the cells' antioxidant defenses, causing the hemoglobin to denature and form clumps known as Heinz bodies3.... This damage leads to the rapid destruction of red blood cells, resulting in life-threatening hemolytic anemia36.
Concentration/Potency notes: These compounds are incredibly potent in cats. Furthermore, processing methods like drying and granulation significantly concentrate these reactive compounds, making powdered and granulated garlic substantially more dangerous and likely to cause oxidative damage than fresh garlic
toxins.concentration_notes: These compounds are incredibly potent in cats. Furthermore, processing methods like drying and granulation significantly concentrate these reactive compounds, making powdered and granulated garlic substantially more dangerous and likely to cause oxidative damage than fresh garlic

toxins.name: Allicin and Ajoene
toxins.description:
These are pharmacologically active agents found specifically in garlic. They act as potent cardiac and smooth muscle relaxants, vasodilators, and hypotensive (blood-pressure-lowering) agents8. Ajoene is additionally a potent antithrombotic (blood-thinning) agent8. While they are not the primary cause of red blood cell destruction, their hypotensive and antithrombotic properties severely exacerbate the physiological effects of the anemia and impaired oxygen transportation caused by the thiosulfates8.
Concentration/Potency notes: These are highly active constituents of garlic oil9. Unaged garlic preparations containing these compounds can also cause direct, painful damage to the gastric and ileal mucosa (stomach and intestinal lining), resulting in severe diarrhea
toxins.concentration_notes: These are highly active constituents of garlic oil9. Unaged garlic preparations containing these compounds can also cause direct, painful damage to the gastric and ileal mucosa (stomach and intestinal lining), resulting in severe diarrhea

toxins.name: Alk(en)yl cysteine sulfoxides (Precursors)
toxins.description:
These are the parent compounds sequestered inside the plant's cells11. When the garlic clove experiences mechanical trauma—such as being chewed by a cat, chopped, or crushed—these precursors instantly convert into the complex mixture of toxic sulfur-containing organic compounds (like allicin and the thiosulfates mentioned above)11.
Concentration/Potency notes: These compounds are responsible for the characteristic odor and flavor of garlic11. Because they are heavily concentrated throughout the plant, any ingestion of garlic exposes the cat to these toxin-generating precursors
toxins.concentration_notes: These compounds are responsible for the characteristic odor and flavor of garlic11. Because they are heavily concentrated throughout the plant, any ingestion of garlic exposes the cat to these toxin-generating precursors

symptoms.name: Because garlic is a member of the Allium family and contains potent organosulfur compounds, it causes symptoms very similar to onion toxicosis
Vomiting, Diarrhea, Abdominal Pain, and Drooling
symptoms.onset: Often within 24 hours, though it can be delayed 2 to 4 days
symptoms.notes: These are typically the first signs of toxicosis to appear2. Unaged garlic preparations contain specific compounds that can cause direct, painful damage to the gastric and ileal mucosa, resulting in severe abdominal pain and diarrhea45. The toxins can also cause direct irritation to the mouth, leading to drooling

symptoms.name: Lethargy, Weakness, and Depression
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days (typically 2 to 4 days)
symptoms.notes: These symptoms emerge as a secondary response to systemic hypoxia caused by the profound destruction of oxygen-carrying red blood cells (hemolytic anemia)

symptoms.name: Pale Mucous Membranes and Jaundice (Icterus)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: Pale gums indicate a lack of oxygenated blood (anemia), while jaundice (yellowing of the skin/mucosa) indicates severe red blood cell destruction leading to an excess of bilirubin in the blood (hyperbilirubinemia)

symptoms.name: Tachypnea and Dyspnea (Breathing Difficulties)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: This is a compensatory response to the decreased blood oxygen transportation capacity and resulting tissue hypoxia

symptoms.name: Tachycardia and Hypotension (Cardiovascular Distress)
symptoms.notes: Tachycardia develops as the heart pumps faster to compensate for impaired oxygen transportation6. Furthermore, garlic contains pharmacologically active agents (allicin and ajoene) that act as potent cardiac and smooth muscle relaxants, vasodilators, and hypotensive agents4. These hypotensive properties severely exacerbate the physiological effects of the anemia

symptoms.name: Hemoglobinuria (Dark Urine)
symptoms.onset: After a lag of several days
symptoms.notes: This is a hallmark sign of severe oxidative hemolysis, where damaged red blood cells rupture and release hemoglobin, which is then excreted through the urinary tract

symptoms.name: Cold Sensitivity
symptoms.notes: Affected cats may have difficulty regulating their body temperature due to the systemic effects of the anemia and hypoxia

symptoms.name: Garlic Breath
symptoms.notes: While not dangerous itself, this symptom is specifically associated with recent ingestion and serves as a highly valuable diagnostic clue for veterinarians

treatments.name: 1. Oxygen Therapy and Blood Transfusions (Life-Saving Stabilization)
treatments.description: of the procedure: Providing the cat with supplemental oxygen and transfusing whole blood to restore the body's depleted oxygen-carrying capacity
treatments.notes: This is the most urgent priority for severely affected cats presenting with life-threatening signs such as respiratory distress, systemic hypoxia, or profound hemolytic anemia1.... Blood transfusions may be specifically indicated if the cat's hematocrit drops below 15–20%3. Because there is no specific antidote for Allium (garlic) toxicosis, stabilizing the respiratory and circulatory systems is critical to prevent fatal collapse

treatments.name: Induction of Emesis (Early Gastrointestinal Decontamination)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: Administering an emetic drug, typically an α
2
	​

-adrenergic agonist like dexmedetomidine (via intramuscular or intravenous injection), to force the cat to expel the ingested garlic before the toxins are absorbed into the bloodstream
treatments.notes: This early decontamination step is highly valuable but only indicated if the ingestion was very recent (typically caught within 1 to 2 hours) and the cat is currently asymptomatic2.... Crucially, 3% hydrogen peroxide must never be used to induce vomiting in cats, as it is strictly contraindicated and can cause fatal hemorrhagic gastritis

treatments.name: Administration of Activated Charcoal (Secondary Decontamination)
treatments.description: of the procedure: Administering oral activated charcoal (typically 1 to 4 g/kg) to bind to and adsorb any residual toxins remaining in the stomach and intestines
treatments.notes: This is typically administered directly after emesis to trap toxins before they can enter the bloodstream1.... However, it should be noted that its actual efficacy in binding to or preventing the production of Allium-derived sulfides is still unknown

treatments.name: Intravenous (IV) Fluid Therapy (Systemic Supportive Care)
treatments.description: of the procedure: Hospitalizing the cat to administer continuous IV fluids to rehydrate the animal, support blood pressure, and maintain proper renal perfusion
treatments.notes: This treatment is indicated if the cat has suffered extensive fluid loss from severe vomiting and diarrhea, or presents with hypotension12. Furthermore, it is a highly recommended protective measure if the cat develops hemoglobinuria (dark urine caused by ruptured red blood cells), as IV diuresis helps protect the kidneys against secondary damage known as hemoglobinuric nephrosis

treatments.name: Gastrointestinal and Antioxidant Medications (Symptomatic Relief)
treatments.description: of the procedure: Administering anti-sickness/anti-nausea medications, stomach protectants, and sometimes antioxidant vitamins (C and E) or N-acetyl-cysteine
treatments.notes: The treatment of garlic poisoning is generally nonspecific and primarily symptomatic11. While antioxidants are sometimes administered in an attempt to combat the oxidative damage to red blood cells, they have shown minimal proven protective effects against Allium toxicity in cats
